fragility_points: A late selloff that pushes Binance BTCUSDT back below 70000 before noon ET.
Higher short-term volatility than the recent sample suggests.
Any operational mismatch between Binance UI settlement display and API data.
key_assumptions: Recent Binance stability above 70000 remains informative through the noon-ET resolution window.
No major macro or crypto-specific selloff hits before resolution.
Binance web-chart settlement values stay aligned with Binance API-visible candle values.
main_logical_chain: Check the contract-defining source to identify the exact pair, exact minute, strict comparison rule, and settlement venue.
Verify direct Binance data for BTCUSDT symbol identity, UTC timing basis, current spot level, and recent 1-minute threshold occupancy.
Compare the comfortable current margin above 70000 with the fact that settlement is a single minute nearly a day later.
Conclude that Yes is still likely but the strongest credible variant is slight market overconfidence rather than a bearish flip.
main_thesis: Yes remains the base case because Binance BTCUSDT is comfortably above 70000 and recent minute-level occupancy above that threshold is strong, but the market may be slightly overconfident because settlement depends on one exact noon-ET minute close tomorrow.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified.
All sampled 1000 one-minute Binance closes were above 70000.
source_quality_view: Primary evidence quality is high because Binance is the named settlement source and Polymarket defines the contract, but independence is only medium-low and there is mild UI/API source-of-truth ambiguity.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact minute close tomorrow, not on today's spot level.
A roughly 3.2% cushion can still be erased by an overnight or morning selloff.
The rules reference the Binance web UI, leaving mild UI-versus-API ambiguity.
strongest_supports: Binance spot was around 72.29k-72.35k during research, roughly 3.2% above the threshold.
All sampled 1000 recent Binance one-minute closes were above 70000.
The market rules clearly identify Binance BTC/USDT 1m close as the governing source.
timing_relevance: Timing is central because the contract resolves on the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle on 2026-04-14, which maps to about 16:00 UTC under DST.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Polymarket would rely on a Binance UI display if it ever diverged from raw API klines.
How stable the current >70000 occupancy remains over the next ~23 hours.
what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if Binance BTCUSDT moved back below 70000 before resolution or if evidence emerged that the settlement chart could diverge from API-visible candle values; I would move higher if BTC stayed comfortably above 71k-72k into late morning ET.
